notes.total: 204 tickets scoped across 7 sprints — healthy pipeline for a 13-week quarter.
notes.completed: 187 of 204 tickets completed (92%), with 13 still in progress at quarter end — strong delivery performance.
notes.completion_rate: 92% completion rate indicates excellent execution with minimal scope left unfinished.
notes.releases_shipped: 16 releases shipped over 7 sprints — strong cadence of 2+ releases per sprint on average.
notes.oos_total: 52 out-of-sprint tickets (25% of total) indicates reactive planning or mid-sprint scope changes — worth reviewing sprint discipline.
notes.oos_open: All out-of-sprint items resolved by quarter end — no unplanned debt carried forward.
notes.type_split: Healthy mix: 45% tasks, 37% stories, 18% bugs — bug ratio is well-controlled and within normal range.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 1 release per sprint with mean of 2.3 suggests some sprints had batch releases while most shipped steadily.
notes.time_logged: 1,876 hours logged against 3,180 estimated (59% utilisation) — significant under-logging or estimates were inflated.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 41% variance (team logged 59% of estimates) indicates chronic over-estimation or incomplete time tracking.
notes.no_estimate: 52 tickets (25%) had no estimate — quarter of the work was unquantified, weakening planning reliability.
notes.rollover: 35 tickets rolled over (17%) — manageable but signals some sprint predictability issues worth addressing.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 25 days with mean of 38 suggests most tickets flow reasonably but outliers extended timelines significantly.
notes.assignee_workload: Core team of 6 carried 123 tickets; Vitomir at 55% completion (11/20) stands out as underperforming while others hit 83-100%.
